stemHtml:
A flight crew of a performance class A aeroplane are preparing for departure on a short and wet runway. Only the Captain has run the performance figures using an application on their electronic flight bag (EFB), and has not crosschecked the results with the first officer, who is busy preparing the departure routing. The Captain has incorrectly calculated a V1 of 118 kt and entered it into the FMS. The VMCG is 120 kt, VR is 123 kt, V2 is 130 kt

During take-off, the pilot monitoring calls V1 at the FMS selected V1 speed, at which point an engine failure occurs. The flight crew continue the takeoff roll.

What would be the most likely scenario in this event?

options.b: It will take off and climb normally
options.c: The aircraft will veer to one side, but takeoff safely
options.d: The aircraft will laterally overrun the runway
